steps.title: Identify where the modifier belongs
steps.content: The phrase “including artists Vasily Kandinsky and Piet Mondrian” clarifies who her contemporaries were. This phrase must be positioned immediately after “her contemporaries” to clearly describe what it is modifying.

steps.title: Apply the correct boundary
steps.content:
A modifying phrase must appear immediately after the noun it describes.

Choice B positions this phrase exactly where it belongs: “...well before many of her contemporaries, including artists...” — the modifier comes right after its noun.

Choices A and C place a comma after “painting” instead, creating a boundary that separates the modifier from the noun it should describe; Choice D contains the spelling error “occured” (should be “occurred”).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
